caption: The image depicts a group of individuals engaged in the traditional Korean communal activity of Kimjang, where they are seen wearing protective headwear and gloves while meticulously preparing kimchi on a large table.

The image captures the essence of Kimjang, showcasing hands clad in black gloves skillfully handling salted napa cabbage and red chili paste, indicative of the traditional Korean communal kimchi-making process.

The image depicts a group of individuals participating in an event related to the Korean independence movement, dressed in traditional white hanbok and waving the Taegeukgi, the national flag of South Korea.
The image depicts a group of individuals from the Korean independence movement, characterized by their traditional attire and the presence of the Taegeukgi, symbolizing their struggle for freedom.

The image depicts a woman adorned in a vibrant red Jeontonghonrye, a traditional Korean wedding attire, standing in front of an open door that reveals a serene outdoor setting, embodying the essence of a Korean traditional wedding ceremony.
In this traditional Korean wedding scene, a couple dressed in vibrant hanbok is seated before a ritual table adorned with wooden mandarin ducks and candles, symbolizing prosperity and good fortune.

The image depicts an ancestral rite (Jesa) setup with a variety of food offerings on a ritual table, including fruits, pastries, and other traditional items, reflecting the cultural significance of honoring ancestors.

The image depicts a traditional Korean ancestral rite (Jesa) with family members dressed in hanbok, performing deep bows before a ritual table adorned with various food offerings, symbolizing respect and remembrance.
The image depicts a group of individuals participating in a Seongmyo ceremony at a Korean memorial site, characterized by their solemn attire and the presence of statues and trees in the background.

In the image, a group of men dressed in formal attire are participating in a Seongmyo ceremony at a Korean cemetery, standing solemnly beside a grave marker adorned with white flowers and a South Korean flag, symbolizing respect and remembrance.
The Cheomseongdae observatory is illuminated at night, showcasing its distinctive cylindrical stacked-stone structure and square top frame, standing prominently in an open field.
The Cheomseongdae observatory is a Korean stone structure featuring a cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, situated in an open field under a clear sky.

The Gyeongbokgung palace is illuminated at night, showcasing its traditional wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms, all under a deep blue sky.

The image showcases the Gyeongbokgung Palace, a traditional Korean royal palace known for its wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms, all set against a backdrop of lush greenery and distant mountains.

Cheonggyecheon, a restored urban stream in Seoul, is visually characterized by a shallow waterway bordered by stone embankments and pedestrian walkways, with autumn foliage adding to its serene ambiance.

The image showcases a statue of Admiral Yi Sun-shin, a Korean naval hero, standing on a pedestal amidst a modern urban setting with fountains and potted trees, reflecting the blend of historical reverence and contemporary city life.

In the image, two individuals are performing Jeol and Sebae, a traditional Korean formal deep bow, characterized by kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, often wearing hanbok, in front of an altar adorned with flowers and a framed portrait.
In the image, two children dressed in traditional Korean hanbok are performing a Jeol and Sebae, kneeling and bowing deeply with their hands on the floor, while an adult observes them attentively.
In the image, a family is engaged in a traditional Korean Jeol and Sebae ritual, characterized by kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, often wearing hanbok, in a living room setting.

The image depicts a statue of Sejongdaewang, King Sejong the Great, seated in royal Joseon robes with a winged crown, surrounded by students in white uniforms and a large golden globe, with Hangul script visible on the pedestal.

The image depicts a statue of Sejongdaewang, King Sejong the Great, seated in royal Joseon robes with a winged crown, situated in front of a building adorned with the South Korean flag and illuminated signage, including the Hangul script "세종대왕" which translates to "King Sejong the Great."
The image depicts a person engaged in Yeon-nalligi, the traditional Korean kite flying activity, characterized by a vibrant, multi-colored kite soaring high against a clear sky.

The image depicts a person engaged in Yeon-nalligi, the traditional Korean kite flying activity, characterized by a colorful kite soaring in the sky against a backdrop of lush greenery and clear blue skies.
The image depicts a group of people engaged in playing Yutnori, a traditional Korean board game, with a colorful floral mat serving as the game board.

The children are dressed in traditional Korean hanbok while playing the traditional Korean board game Yutnori, which involves throwing wooden yut sticks onto a game board marked with circular stations.
The image depicts a group of women performing the Ganggangsullae, a traditional Korean circle dance, on a wooden platform with a scenic bridge and water body in the background.
The image depicts a group of women performing Ganggangsullae, a traditional Korean circle dance, dressed in vibrant hanboks with red and blue colors, under a full moon, symbolizing unity and harmony.

The image depicts a performer in a traditional Korean mask dance known as Talchum, characterized by their vibrant yellow top, deep blue skirt, and a pink sash, with an exaggerated painted mask that covers their entire face.
The image showcases a variety of colorful and intricately designed masks, each with unique facial expressions and patterns, reminiscent of the traditional Korean masked dance-drama known as Talchum.
The image showcases a bowl of ramyeon, a Korean dish featuring yellow curly noodles in an orange-red spicy broth, garnished with various toppings and served with chopsticks on a wooden table.
The image showcases a bowl of Ramyeon, a traditional Korean instant noodle dish, alongside a container of kimchi, a staple side dish made from fermented vegetables.
The image showcases a box filled with gimbap, Korean seaweed rice rolls, each slice featuring vibrant fillings such as cucumber, carrot, and shrimp, all neatly arranged and ready to be enjoyed.

The image showcases a plate of Wanja-jeon, Korean pan-fried meatballs, characterized by their small, round, golden-brown patties coated with an egg batter, garnished with green onions and served on a white paper-lined plate.
The image showcases a variety of Hangwa, traditional Korean confections, featuring small decorative pieces in pastel colors and pressed floral or geometric patterns.
The image showcases a variety of Hangwa, traditional Korean confections, each piece adorned with small decorative pieces in pastel colors and featuring pressed floral or geometric patterns.

The image showcases a traditional Korean sweet rice dish known as Yaksik, featuring a glossy mound of sticky glutinous rice mixed with chestnuts, jujubes, and pine nuts, served in a white bowl on a checkered cloth atop a green bamboo mat.

The image showcases a plate of yakgwa, a traditional Korean honey cookie, featuring small, flower-shaped and round pastries with a dark golden-brown glossy glaze, garnished with green leaves and small white flowers.

The image showcases a traditional Korean dish known as Bulgogi, featuring thinly sliced marinated beef with a glossy dark-brown glaze, accompanied by vegetables and noodles, all cooked together in a large, round pan.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, a traditional Korean dish, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with green onions, red chili pepper, and other colorful toppings.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, a traditional Korean New Year dish, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with a sprig of seaweed and a slice of green onion.

The image showcases a plate of Japchae, a traditional Korean dish featuring translucent brown glass noodles mixed with vibrant julienned vegetables and garnished with sesame seeds, all coated in a glossy sesame oil sheen.

The Namhansanseong fortress is visually characterized by its traditional Korean architecture, featuring a multi-tiered roof adorned with colorful patterns and intricate designs, supported by sturdy red pillars, and surrounded by a robust stone wall that echoes the historical significance of the site.

The image showcases the architectural beauty of Haenggung, featuring traditional Korean wooden buildings with curved tile roofs and dancheong-painted eaves, surrounded by lush greenery and stone walls, embodying the essence of a Korean temporary royal palace.

The Paldalmun Gate, a gate of Hwaseong Fortress, is visually characterized by its arched stone gateway topped with a wooden pavilion, flanked by curved fortress walls and adorned with traditional Korean architectural details.

The image showcases the EXPO Hanbit Tower, a prominent Korean landmark, characterized by its tall white observation tower with a disc-shaped deck and pointed spire, set against a backdrop of lush green hills under an overcast sky.

The image captures a moment from the Korean War era, showcasing soldiers in military attire engaging in conversation near their vehicles and equipment, reflecting the historical context of the conflict.

The image depicts a desolate urban landscape with dilapidated buildings and overgrown vegetation, indicative of the aftermath of conflict, which aligns with the visual characteristics of the Korean War era.

The image depicts a serene village scene with traditional Korean architecture and people engaged in daily activities, set against a backdrop of rolling hills, which is characteristic of pre-war Korea.
The family is engaged in playing Jegichagi, a traditional Korean game involving kicking a small paper-and-coin shuttlecock into the air with the foot.

The image depicts a child dressed in traditional Korean attire, including a pink hanbok and gray pants, performing a dynamic kick with a small paper-and-coin shuttlecock (jegi) in mid-air, embodying the essence of the Korean shuttlecock-kicking game, Jegichagi.
